body:
Implements [WOR-102](https://alphapoint.atlassian.net/browse/WOR-102): **App shell — Vite + React + ConvexProvider + AuthProvider + routing**

## Task Description

This task establishes the frontend application skeleton that every subsequent UI task builds on. It wires up the Vite dev server, React 18 entry point, Convex reactive backend connection (ConvexProvider), authentication layer (Convex Auth provider), and the full React Router v6 route tree defined in TechSpec §9.2. Without this shell, no frontend page can render, authenticate, or subscribe to live data. The task also includes the TopNav component (logged-in and case-detail variants per DesignDoc §3.2) and a frontend error handler utility that maps Convex error codes to user-friendly toast messages.

Acceptance Criteria

main.tsx renders React 18 with ConvexProvider and AuthProvider wrapping the router



App.tsx defines all routes from TechSpec §9.2: /, /login, /invite/:token, /dashboard, /cases/new, /cases/:caseId, /cases/:caseId/private, /cases/:caseId/joint, /cases/:caseId/closed, /admin/templates, /admin/templates/:id, /admin/audit



Protected routes redirect to /login when unauthenticated



Admin routes (/admin/*) redirect to /dashboard for non-admin users



TopNav renders the logged-in variant with Dashboard link and user menu, and the case-detail variant with back arrow and phase display per DesignDoc §3.2



Navigation between routes works with browser back/forward buttons



Vite config includes VITE_CONVEX_URL environment variable



Frontend error handler utility maps ConvexError codes (UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, etc.) to user-friendly toast messages





Implementation Notes

**Tech stack:** React 18, Vite, TypeScript, React Router v6, Convex (convex/react hooks), @convex-dev/auth for auth provider.



**File structure (TechSpec §9.1):** src/main.tsx (entry point with providers), src/App.tsx (route definitions), src/components/layout/TopNav.tsx, src/lib/ for error handler utility.



**Provider nesting order:** <ConvexProvider> → <ConvexAuthProvider> → <BrowserRouter> → <Routes>. The Convex URL comes from import.meta.env.VITE_CONVEX_URL.



**Protected route pattern:** A wrapper component checks useConvexAuth() — if isLoading, show spinner; if !isAuthenticated, <Navigate to="/login" replace />.



**Admin route guard:** After auth check, query the current user's role. If role !== "ADMIN", redirect to /dashboard.



**TopNav variants:** Logged-out (minimal), logged-in (Dashboard link + user menu), case-detail (back arrow via ArrowLeft icon at 14px + phase display). Conditional rendering based on route and auth state.



**Error handler utility (TechSpec §7.4):** Maps the 9 Convex error codes (UNAUTHENTICATED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, CONFLICT, INVALID_INPUT, TOKEN_INVALID, RATE_LIMITED, AI_ERROR, INTERNAL) to user-friendly messages for toast display. Wrap in a handleConvexError(error) function.



**Route pages:** Use placeholder/stub components for pages not yet implemented — just enough to render a heading and confirm routing works. Actual page content is built in later tasks.



**Accessibility (NFR-A11Y):** On route change, focus should move to the page's <h1>. Correct landmarks (<main>, <nav>). Focus rings via :focus-visible.



**Browser support (NFR-BROWSER):** Vite build target es2020. No IE or legacy browser support needed.



**Dependencies:** Depends on P0.1 (project scaffolding/Vite init), T1 (Convex schema — needed for auth/user types), and T9 (theme/style setup — needed for TopNav styling).





Test-Gen Brief

**AC: main.tsx renders providers** — Integration test (Playwright): load the app root URL, verify the page renders without console errors. Assert that the Convex connection initializes (no "failed to connect" errors).



**AC: Route definitions** — E2E test (Playwright): navigate to each route path and verify the correct page component renders (check for page heading or unique test-id). Cover: /, /login, /dashboard, /cases/new, /admin/templates, /admin/audit.



**AC: Protected routes redirect** — E2E test (Playwright): in an unauthenticated browser context, navigate to /dashboard and assert redirect to /login. Same for /cases/new, /admin/templates.



**AC: Admin routes redirect non-admins** — E2E test (Playwright): log in as a regular USER, navigate to /admin/templates, assert redirect to /dashboard.



**AC: TopNav variants** — E2E test (Playwright): verify logged-in TopNav shows Dashboard link and user menu. On a case detail page, verify back arrow and phase display are present.



**AC: Browser back/forward** — E2E test (Playwright): navigate /dashboard → /cases/new → browser back → assert on /dashboard → browser forward → assert on /cases/new.



**AC: VITE_CONVEX_URL** — Unit test (Vitest): verify vite.config.ts is valid and doesn't error. The env var itself is a runtime config, tested implicitly by E2E.



**AC: Error handler utility** — Unit test (Vitest): import the error handler, pass each of the 9 ConvexError codes, assert the returned message is a non-empty user-friendly string (not the raw code). Test unknown code falls back to a generic message. Fixtures: mock ConvexError objects with each code.





Traceability

Decomposed from Epic WOR-90 plan task_id T8. Depends on plan task_ids: P0.1, T1, T9.

## Validation

All quality gates (lint, typecheck, scoped unit tests, scoped E2E tests) passed locally before this PR was opened.

---

🤖 *Autonomously generated by the Archon pipeline. Tests written by `task-tests`; implementation written by `task-implement`.*

comments.body:
# Scope Review Findings: PR #14

**Reviewer**: scope-review
**Date**: 2026-05-15T00:00:00Z
**Files Reviewed**: 20

## Summary
All 20 changed files are directly relevant to the WOR-102 app shell ticket. Every acceptance criterion is addressed by the diff. Two minor scope observations: a small README "Getting Started" addition not called for by any AC, and a fix to a pre-existing WOR-103 test file — both are low-impact.
**Verdict**: APPROVE

## Findings

### Finding 1: README "Getting Started" section added
**Severity**: LOW
**Category**: ac-overreach
**Location**: `README.md:17-35`
**Issue**: The diff adds a "Getting Started" section with install/env-var/dev-server instructions. No AC mentions updating the README.
**Evidence**: `README.md` — 19 new lines describing `npm install`, `.env.local`, and dev commands.
**Why This Matters**: Minimal risk — it's accurate, small, and arguably expected for a task that introduces the dev-server entry point. Not worth blocking over.

### Finding 2: WOR-103 test file modified
**Severity**: LOW
**Category**: unrelated-change
**Location**: `tests/unit/theme-tokens.test.ts:443-448`
**Issue**: The `theme-tokens.test.ts` file (shipped by WOR-103) is modified to wrap `imported.default.light`/`.dark` in `Object.fromEntries`. This appears to be a compatibility fix discovered while getting the test suite running with the new app shell dependencies.
**Evidence**: `tests/unit/theme-tokens.test.ts` — 6-line change converting dynamic import result to plain Record.
**Why This Matters**: Low risk. The change is a narrow bug fix to an existing test, not a behavioral change to theme logic. It does not alter WOR-103's production code.

## Statistics
Total findings: 2
- CRITICAL: 0
- HIGH: 0
- MEDIUM: 0
- LOW: 2

# Comprehensive PR Review

**PR**: #14 — WOR-102: App shell (Vite + React + ConvexProvider + AuthProvider + routing)
**Reviewed by**: 5 specialized agents (code-review, error-handling, test-coverage, comment-quality, docs-impact)
**Date**: 2026-05-15

---

## Summary

Solid app shell with correct provider nesting, well-structured route guards, comprehensive error handler, and thorough documentation. Two HIGH issues and two MEDIUM issues should be addressed before merge. Overall code quality is high.

**Verdict**: `REQUEST_CHANGES`

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| HIGH | 2 |
| MEDIUM | 2 |
| LOW | 5 |

---

## HIGH Issues

### 1. Non-null assertion on \`identity.email\` in \`convex/users.ts\`

**\`convex/users.ts:12\`** — *code-review*

\`identity.email!\` assumes email is always present, but \`UserIdentity.email\` is \`string | undefined\`. If a non-email auth provider is used, this silently passes \`undefined\` to the \`by_email\` index. This query runs on every admin route render.

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

\`\`\`typescript
const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity();
if (!identity || !identity.email) {
  return null;
}
const user = await ctx.db
  .query("users")
  .withIndex("by_email", (q) => q.eq("email", identity.email))
  .unique();
\`\`\`

</details>

---

### 2. Provider nesting in docs/contract doesn't match actual code

**\`docs/app-shell.md:103-109\`, \`docs/contracts/wor-102.md:222-223\`** — *comment-quality, code-review*

Docs describe \`<ConvexProvider>\` as a separate wrapper, but the code only uses \`<ConvexAuthProvider client={convex}>\` (which wraps ConvexProvider internally). Contract signature comment also has the wrong prop target.

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

Update \`docs/app-shell.md\` provider stack:
\`\`\`
<ConvexAuthProvider>      — wraps ConvexProvider internally; provides
                            reactive backend connection (VITE_CONVEX_URL)
                            + session / identity management
  <BrowserRouter>         — client-side routing
    <App />
\`\`\`

Update \`docs/contracts/wor-102.md\` signature:
\`\`\`typescript
/* src/main.tsx — no named exports; side-effect module */
// Renders: <ConvexAuthProvider client={convex}> → <BrowserRouter> → <App />
\`\`\`

</details>

---

## MEDIUM Issues (Needs Decision)

### 1. Unsafe VITE_CONVEX_URL cast with no validation

**\`src/main.tsx:8\`** — *error-handling*

\`import.meta.env.VITE_CONVEX_URL as string\` with no runtime check. Missing env var produces a blank white page with cryptic SDK error.

**Options**: Fix now (3 lines) | Create issue | Skip

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

\`\`\`typescript
const convexUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_CONVEX_URL;
if (!convexUrl) {
  throw new Error("Missing VITE_CONVEX_URL environment variable");
}
const convex = new ConvexReactClient(convexUrl);
\`\`\`

</details>

---

### 2. E2E test uses wrong data-testid

**\`e2e/app-shell.spec.ts:584\`** — *comment-quality*

Test checks \`data-testid='dashboard-page'\` but the stub uses \`data-testid="page-dashboard"\`. Assertion passes trivially (element never found), giving false confidence.

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

\`\`\`typescript
const dashboardContent = page.locator("[data-testid='page-dashboard']");
\`\`\`

</details>

---

## LOW Issues

<details>
<summary>View 5 low-priority suggestions</summary>

| # | Issue | Location | Agent | Suggestion |
|---|-------|----------|-------|------------|
| 1 | Login route flashes for authenticated users during loading | \`src/App.tsx:94\` | code-review | Add \`isLoading\` check before auth redirect |
| 2 | Duplicated loading spinner markup (3 instances) | \`ProtectedRoute.tsx\`, \`AdminRoute.tsx\` | code-review | Defer extraction to future PR |
| 3 | AdminRoute conflates null-user with non-admin | \`AdminRoute.tsx:33\` | error-handling | Acceptable for scaffold; revisit later |
| 4 | handleConvexError swallows original error context | \`errorHandler.ts:18-27\` | error-handling | Keep pure; document caller logging responsibility |
| 5 | Red-state comments in test files outdated | \`e2e/app-shell.spec.ts:448\`, \`errorHandler.test.ts:1437\` | comment-quality | Remove red-state paragraphs |

</details>

---

## What's Good

- **Clean route guard architecture** — correctly handles loading, unauthenticated, and authorized states without content flashes
- **AdminRoute correctly orders checks** — auth verified before querying user role
- **Error handler is well-designed** — covers all 9 error codes, never leaks internals, thorough test coverage
- **TopNav is genuinely presentational** — all data via props, no hooks
- **Proactive documentation** — ships with \`docs/app-shell.md\`, updated \`docs/errors.md\`, thorough contract, changelog, and README Getting Started
- **\`replace\` on all redirects** — prevents back-button loops
- **AdminRoute skips query when unauthenticated** — idiomatic Convex \`"skip"\` pattern

---

## Suggested Follow-up Issues

| Issue Title | Priority |
|-------------|----------|
| Extract shared LoadingSpinner component | P3 |
| Add frontend error logging infrastructure | P3 |
| Separate null-user from non-admin in AdminRoute | P3 |

---

## Next Steps

1. Auto-fix step will address HIGH + MEDIUM issues
2. Review LOW issues and decide: fix now, create issue, or skip
3. Merge when ready

---

*Reviewed by Archon comprehensive-pr-review workflow*
*Artifacts: \`artifacts/runs/03c9d80c1026d975363261233508c436/review/\`*

# Fix Report: PR #14

**Date**: 2026-05-15T03:00:00Z
**Branch**: archon/task-wor-102
**Commit**: c59e3645e4e226204a2d8dfc0c37ae2ef2379fbf

---

## Fixes applied (7)

| Severity | Issue | Location | What changed |
|----------|-------|----------|--------------|
| HIGH | Non-null assertion on `identity.email` could fail with non-email auth providers | `convex/users.ts:10-12` | Removed `!` assertion; added early `return null` guard when `identity.email` is undefined |
| HIGH | Provider nesting in docs/contract doesn't match actual code (describes separate `<ConvexProvider>` wrapper) | `docs/app-shell.md:11-17`, `docs/contracts/wor-102.md:53,84,139,186` | Updated all references to reflect that `<ConvexAuthProvider>` wraps `ConvexProvider` internally |
| MEDIUM | `VITE_CONVEX_URL` cast to `string` with no runtime validation — blank page on missing env var | `src/main.tsx:8` | Added runtime guard that throws a clear error message when env var is missing |
| LOW | Login route redirects authenticated users during loading state, causing flash | `src/App.tsx:95` | Added `!isLoading` check before auth redirect on `/login` route |
| LOW | Duplicated loading spinner markup across ProtectedRoute and AdminRoute (3 instances) | `src/components/layout/ProtectedRoute.tsx`, `src/components/layout/AdminRoute.tsx` | Extracted shared `LoadingSpinner` component into `src/components/layout/LoadingSpinner.tsx` |
| LOW | AdminRoute conflates null-user (no DB record) with non-admin into single check | `src/components/layout/AdminRoute.tsx:33` | Split `!user \|\| user.role !== "ADMIN"` into separate `null` and role checks for clearer control flow |
| LOW | `handleConvexError` intentionally swallows original error context but this isn't documented | `src/lib/errorHandler.ts:18` | Added JSDoc explaining the design choice and that callers should log before calling |

---

## Deferred (questions for human review) (0)

No findings were categorized as questions — all were actionable issues.

---

## Deferred (pipeline-restricted) (2)

| Severity | Issue | Location | Reason |
|----------|-------|----------|--------|
| MEDIUM | E2E spec references `data-testid='dashboard-page'` but stub uses `page-dashboard` | `e2e/app-shell.spec.ts:584` | Test files are owned by a separate pipeline step |
| LOW | Outdated red-state comments in spec files | `e2e/app-shell.spec.ts:448`, `errorHandler.test.ts:1437` | Test files are owned by a separate pipeline step |

commits.messageHeadline: [test-gen] WOR-102: tests for "App shell — Vite + React + ConvexProvi…

commits.messageBody:
…outing

Implement the full frontend app shell:
- main.tsx: React 18 entry with ConvexAuthProvider + BrowserRouter
- App.tsx: Complete route tree (/, /login, /invite/:token, /dashboard,
  /cases/*, /admin/*) with stub page components
- ProtectedRoute: Auth guard using useConvexAuth, redirects to /login
- AdminRoute: Role-based guard querying api.users.me, redirects non-admins
- TopNav: Presentational component with logged-in and case-detail variants
- errorHandler.ts: Maps 9 ConvexError codes to user-friendly messages
- convex/users.ts: Minimal "me" query for role checking
- vite-env.d.ts: Vite client type declarations

Note: ConvexAuthProvider from @convex-dev/auth replaces ConvexProvider
(takes client prop directly) rather than nesting inside it, per the
library's actual API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: WOR-102: App shell — Vite + React + ConvexProvider + AuthProvider + r…

commits.messageBody:
Replace dual-route-tree pattern with single route tree so that
ProtectedRoute/AdminRoute handle auth gating. Previously, AppLayout
checked only isAuthenticated (not isLoading), causing protected routes
to redirect to /login during auth initialization instead of showing a
spinner. Now the single Routes tree always renders, and each guard
component correctly shows a spinner while isLoading is true.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: WOR-102: Fix loading-state redirect bug in AppLayout
WOR-102: Add docs for app shell, routing, and frontend error handler

commits.messageBody:
Fixes applied:
- HIGH-1: Remove non-null assertion on identity.email in convex/users.ts, add early return if email missing
- HIGH-2: Correct provider nesting docs in app-shell.md and contract to match actual ConvexAuthProvider usage
- MEDIUM-1: Add runtime validation for VITE_CONVEX_URL env var in src/main.tsx
- LOW-1: Add isLoading check on login route to prevent flash redirect during auth loading
- LOW-2: Extract shared LoadingSpinner component to deduplicate spinner markup across route guards
- LOW-3: Separate null-user from non-admin checks in AdminRoute for clearer control flow
- LOW-4: Add JSDoc to handleConvexError documenting caller logging responsibility

Deferred (pipeline-restricted, not addressable from this node):
- MEDIUM-2: Mismatched data-testid in E2E spec
- LOW-5: Outdated red-state comments in spec files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: WOR-102: App shell — Vite + React + ConvexProvider + AuthProvider + routing
